steps.title: Read the artist's thought
steps.content: The artist proudly thinks, “And surely this shall last!” and “Fixed in immortal marble is my fame!” Those lines show that he expects the sculpture to preserve his reputation over time.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported ideas
steps.content: Choice A adds praise for intricate details, which the poem does not state. Choice B invents future repairs. Choice D claims the sculpture surpasses other Venus images, but no comparison appears.

steps.title: Connect time and fame
steps.content: The remaining choice states the artist's belief that the sculpture would endure and bring him lasting fame. The answer is C.
